requirement: Build a minimal HTTP server that responds to GET / with a Hello World JSON message and GET /health with a status check, backed by Zod-validated config and pino structured logging.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: GET / returns HTTP 200 with body { "message": "Hello, World!" }
Unknown routes return HTTP 404 with body { "code": "NOT_FOUND", "message": "..." }
PORT is loaded from .env via Zod schema; defaults to 3000 when not set; CONFIG_INVALID is thrown on invalid values
Every request log entry includes requestId, method, url, and component fields

approach: Node.js built-in http module with no HTTP framework. Zod validates process.env at startup. pino provides structured JSON logs. AppErrorCode enum for all error identifiers. createApp() factory returns start/stop controls for testability without process coupling.
constraintsDiscovered: distroless runner has no shell -- CMD must reference dist/index.js directly without a node prefix
dotenv must be imported before zod schema parse to ensure process.env is populated

risks.risk: Node built-in http has no built-in body parsing or routing -- acceptable for hello world scope
risks.mitigation: Scope is intentionally minimal; framework can be added in a later phase if needed
